tasks.subject: Task 1: fem_mesh.py — Mesh + build_mesh + sliver guard
tasks.description:
**Goal:** A `Mesh` value object (PointSet-like `.points()`), a `build_mesh` Delaunay constructor, and `assert_mesh_quality` (the sliver guard).

**Files:**
- Create: `src/sverdrup/methods/fem_mesh.py`
- Test: `tests/test_fem_mesh.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `build_mesh(points)` triangulates a 2-D point set -> `Mesh` with `points_xy (n,2)`, `triangles (n_tri,3)`, `.points()` returning `(n,3)`.
- [ ] `build_mesh(points, boundary_ring=ring)` includes ring nodes.
- [ ] `assert_mesh_quality` raises on a sliver, passes on a good mesh.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_mesh.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/sverdrup/methods/fem_mesh.py", "tests/test_fem_mesh.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_mesh.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["build_mesh triangulates and Mesh.points() returns (n,3) lon/lat/time", "boundary_ring adds ring nodes", "assert_mesh_quality raises on sliver, passes on good mesh"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: fem_precision + AGNOSTICISM #1 (exact selective-inverse on adversarial mesh)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The P1 SPDE alpha=2 precision assembly + the PRIMARY agnosticism test: `diag(Q^-1)` via the inherited `GMRFFactor.selective_inverse_diag` equals dense `Q^-1` diagonal to rtol 1e-9 on a maximally-adversarial mesh, and every mesh edge is present-and-exact in the selective-inverse pattern.

**Files:**
- Create: `src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py` (fem_precision only)
- Test: `tests/test_fem_precision.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `fem_precision(mesh, kappa, tau)` returns symmetric SPD CSC `Q = (1/tau)(k^2 C + G) C^-1 (k^2 C + G)`.
- [ ] On the adversarial mesh, `GMRFFactor(Q).selective_inverse_diag()` matches dense diag to rtol 1e-9.
- [ ] Every mesh edge in-pattern (0 absent) and exact vs dense.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_precision.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py", "tests/test_fem_precision.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_precision.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["fem_precision returns symmetric SPD CSC Q", "selective_inverse_diag exact vs dense rtol 1e-9 on adversarial mesh", "every mesh edge in selective-inverse pattern (0 absent) and exact"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: FEMBasisProjection — P1 basis read-off conforming to Projection
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The mesh analogue of `bilinear_weights`: a P1 basis projection via `Delaunay.find_simplex` + barycentric coords, conforming to `Projection`, with a mesh-edge-in-pattern `assert_adjacency` (NOT the grid 5-point guard).

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py` (add FEMBasisProjection)
- Test: `tests/test_fem_projection.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `isinstance(FEMBasisProjection(mesh), Projection)` true.
- [ ] `weights(pts)` sparse `(k, n_nodes)`, rows sum to 1; node query is unit selector.
- [ ] `field_shape() == (n_nodes,)`; `node_points(t)` `(n_nodes,3)`.
- [ ] `assert_adjacency(Q)` passes on real Q, raises on missing edge.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_projection.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py", "tests/test_fem_projection.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_projection.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["isinstance(proj, Projection) true", "weights rows sum to 1; node query is unit selector", "field_shape (n_nodes,); node_points (n_nodes,3)", "assert_adjacency passes on real Q, raises on missing edge"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: FEMMatern.solve + register fem + AGNOSTICISM #2 (whole-path grid-shortcut audit)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `FEMMatern.solve` (mirror of `MaternGMRF.solve`, mesh-injected), register `"fem"`, + the second primary agnosticism test: full `solve -> reduce -> project` on an injected adversarial mesh with `bilinear_weights`/`(ny,nx)` reshape guarded to raise if hit, + end-to-end exact marginal var through `GMRFPrecisionReduction` vs dense.

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py` (add FEMMatern)
- Modify: `src/sverdrup/methods/registry.py` (register fem)
- Test: `tests/test_fem_agnosticism_path.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `FEMMatern(mesh=None)` constructible no-args; `.solve(obs, grid, params, time_days)` mirrors MaternGMRF, flat `(n_nodes,)` mean.
- [ ] `"fem"` in `registry.METHODS`.
- [ ] Whole-path run never calls `bilinear_weights`; `field_shape()` `(n_nodes,)`.
- [ ] End-to-end marginal_variance exact vs dense rtol 1e-9.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_agnosticism_path.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["src/sverdrup/methods/fem.py", "src/sverdrup/methods/registry.py", "tests/test_fem_agnosticism_path.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_agnosticism_path.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["FEMMatern(mesh=None).solve mirrors MaternGMRF, flat (n_nodes,) mean", "fem registered in METHODS", "whole-path solve->reduce->project never calls bilinear_weights; field_shape (n_nodes,)", "end-to-end marginal_variance exact vs dense rtol 1e-9"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: C7 boundary-extension mechanism demo vs named grid baseline
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Demonstrate boundary extension on a synthetic irregular-boundary fixture: `edge_var(FEM) < edge_var(grid)` at near-boundary nodes, vs the named baseline (grid GMRF 5-point Neumann-edge Laplacian, `_laplacian`, gmrf_grid.py:48). Mechanism demo, NOT a value claim; not rigged.

**Files:**
- Test: `tests/test_fem_boundary_payoff.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] FEM near-boundary marginal var < grid var at domain-edge nodes (identical kappa/tau/obs).
- [ ] Comparison at near-boundary nodes (not interior-averaged); Neumann-edge baseline documented.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_boundary_payoff.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/test_fem_boundary_payoff.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_boundary_payoff.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["FEM near-boundary marginal var < grid var at domain-edge nodes", "comparison at near-boundary nodes, Neumann-edge grid baseline documented"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: Multi-tile FEM inheritance + C6 shared-node (secondary)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Confirm FEM tiles blend through the live `GmrfTreeKrigingSolve` unchanged, and the discretization-agnostic `CoherenceFeasibility` envelope applies verbatim (MARGINAL_VARIANCE ships; SAMPLES/COVARIANCE only at n_tiles <= n_star_joint). Completeness, not an agnosticism test.

**Files:**
- Test: `tests/test_fem_multitile.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Two overlapping FEM tiles share non-empty coincident-node set (`_tile_adjacency`) — C6.
- [ ] `GmrfTreeKrigingSolve().crossfaded_member(...)` returns finite coherent field.
- [ ] `CoherenceFeasibility` verdict same for mesh as grid: feasible N=1, infeasible N=2.

**Verify:** `pixi run test tests/test_fem_multitile.py -v && pixi run typecheck`

```json:metadata
{"files": ["tests/test_fem_multitile.py"], "verifyCommand": "pixi run test tests/test_fem_multitile.py -v && pixi run typecheck", "acceptanceCriteria": ["two overlapping FEM tiles share non-empty coincident-node set (C6)", "GmrfTreeKrigingSolve.crossfaded_member returns finite coherent field", "CoherenceFeasibility verdict same for mesh as grid (feasible N=1, infeasible N=2)"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```
